description: The first duty of the government is to keep citizens safe and the country secure. The Home Office plays a fundamental role in the security and economic prosperity of the UK. Home Office is a ministerial department, supported by 30 agencies and public bodies .

details.body:
The first duty of the government is to keep citizens safe and the country secure. The Home Office plays a fundamental role in the security and economic prosperity of the UK.

Home Office is a ministerial department, supported by 30 agencies and public bodies.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Lloyds Bank and the government are urging fans to Stop! Think Fraud ahead of the FIFA World Cup next month.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Football fans urged to show fraudsters the red card ahead of World Cup

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Biggest crime-fighting shake up in decades as police gain new powers to tackle local crime as the landmark Crime and Policing Bill receives Royal Assent.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Biggest shake up in decades to tackle local crime

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Information on where to seek advice and assistance if you, or someone you know, has been affected by a terrorist incident.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Support for victims and survivors of terrorism

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New agreement between the UK and France to step up patrols and intelligence operations in France to reduce illegal crossings.
ordered_featured_documents.title: New UK-France agreement to reduce illegal crossings

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Eleven more asylum hotels have been returned to communities nationwide, with further closures coming soon as the Home Office ends their use for good.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Asylum hotels close as government scales up use of large sites

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New guidance published to help organisations and businesses prepare for terrorist threats and protect lives.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Martyn's Law guidance published to help businesses

social_media_links.title: Home Office in the Media blog

details.body:
The role of Permanent Secretary carries with it the responsibility of Principal Accounting Officer, ensuring that the resources authorised by Parliament are used for the purposes authorised by Parliament.

Other responsibilities include:

  • agreeing with the Home Secretary the overarching strategy for delivering ministerial objectives
  • leading, and managing the department to ensure that it operates as effectively and efficiently as possible
  • reporting to the Home Secretary and to Parliament on overall performance

role_appointments.title: Gareth Davies CB - Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Permanent Secretary is responsible for leading the Department for Business and Trade, and is the Secretary of State’s principal policy adviser.

role_appointments.title: Gareth Davies CB - Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Permanent Secretary is responsible for leading the Department for International Trade, and is the Secretary of State’s principal policy adviser.

role_appointments.title: Gareth Davies CB - Permanent Secretary

details.body:
The Second Permanent Secretary General:

  • works with the Permanent Secretary on all aspects of the department’s leadership
  • has specific responsibility for cross-cutting issues such as decarbonisation, security and economic growth
  • is responsible for the department’s work with the aviation and maritime sectors

role_appointments.title: Gareth Davies CB - Second Permanent Secretary

details.body:
The main areas of responsibility are:

  • ensuring that the development of a post-pandemic international travel system supports the UK global ambitions
  • aviation policy including airports, environment, consumer protection, security and safety
  • maritime policy including infrastructure, environment, security and trade
  • space launch
  • security and safety of the transport network
  • global leadership in international aviation, as the UK Director General for Civil Aviation, including for the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO), G7 and Eurocontrol
  • international negotiations with other governments and international transport organisations
  • sponsorship of arm’s length bodies including the CAA, Maritime and Coastguard Agency and the Accident Investigation Branches

role.title: Director General Aviation, Maritime, International and Security

details.body:
The Director General sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards and chairs the Digital and Data Committee.

The Director General is responsible for leading the group’s work to stimulate economic growth through research, innovation and commercialisation. He also leads on start ups, disruptive businesses and enterprise.

role.title: Director General, Business and Science, BIS

role_appointments.title: Gareth Davies - Director General, Business and Science, BIS

details.body:
The Executive Director leads the Government Innovation Group, which works across Whitehall to develop and implement new ways of solving problems. It includes transparency and open data, analysis and insight, open policy making, behavioural insights and the Office for Civil Society.

His primary responsibility is leading the Civil Society agenda, in particular setting up the National Citizen Service for 16/17 year olds, encouraging social action and growing the social investment market by establishing Big Society Capital.

role_appointments.title: Gareth Davies - Executive Director, Government Innovation Group
Simon Ridley CB - Acting Permanent Secretary
Simon Ridley CB - Acting Permanent Secretary

details.body:
Simon leads the Migration and Borders System.

The Migration and Borders System is made up of:

  • Migration and Borders Group
  • Strategic Operations for Illegal Migration
  • Customer Services
  • Immigration Enforcement
  • Border Force

Simon line manages the Migration and Borders directors general.

role_appointments.title: Simon Ridley - Second Permanent Secretary

details.body:
Simon leads the cross-government taskforce on the humanitarian response to the Ukraine refugee crisis.

Simon reports jointly to the Permanent Secretary at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC) and Matthew Rycroft at the Home Office.

The cross-government taskforce is responsible for the delivery of a single end-to-end humanitarian plan.

The role of the taskforce includes:

  • oversight of the UK’s programme of humanitarian assistance for Ukraine
  • working across Whitehall to make sure all departments are supporting the government’s new visa routes and planning for the next steps in the programme
  • undertaking contingency planning across government for possible deterioration of the humanitarian situation
  • making sure there is one consistent narrative for the government’s overall humanitarian response

role.title: Second Permanent Secretary, Home Office and DLUHC

role_appointments.title: Simon Ridley - Second Permanent Secretary

details.body:
The Chief Operating Officer has overall responsibility for a range of the Department for Business and Trade’s (DBT) corporate functions and support services, including:

  • project delivery and change
  • finance

role_appointments.title: Tara Smith - Chief Operating Officer

details.body:
The Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function (GFF) co-leads and develops the community of finance professionals across government, to help to maintain the highest standards of financial management and ensures that public money is spent efficiently and effectively.

Key responsibilities include:

  • setting and delivering a GFF strategy for the development of the Government Finance Function
  • overseeing the professional development and performance of the GFF, including the Government Finance Academy curriculum and the annual continuing professional development plan
  • supporting departments’ talent management, succession planning and recruitment for the most senior financial roles in government

The Joint-Head of the GFF is accountable to Parliamentary select committees, including the Public Accounts Committee, for the continuing improvement of financial management standards, skills and expertise in government.

role.title: Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: Tara Smith - Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

details.body:
The Director General is responsible for:

  • leading on developing, co-ordinating and delivering the government’s strategy, policy and legislation on crime, policing, fire, international law enforcement and judicial cooperation
  • creating the Emergency Services Network, a 4G digital critical communications network which will transform how Britain’s emergency and rescue services operate, and leading various other national digital law enforcement programmes
  • leading the policy and operational teams working on modern slavery and human trafficking, including the UK’s National Referral Mechanism and provision of support for victims in England and Wales

The DG also serves as a member of the Home Office Executive Committee.

role_appointments.title: Richard Clarke - Director General, Public Safety

details.body:
The main areas of responsibility are:

  • aviation policy including airports, airspace, environment, consumers, international connectivity, future of flight, security and safety
  • maritime policy including infrastructure, environment, trade, skills, security and safety
  • aviation, maritime and borders analysis
  • security and resilience of the transport network
  • global leadership on international aviation and maritime, including through the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO), EUROCONTROL and the International Maritime Organization (IMO)

role.title: Director General Aviation, Maritime and Security Group

role_appointments.title: Dr Rannia Leontaridi OBE FRSA - Director General Aviation, Maritime and Security Group

details.body:
The Asylum Taskforce, with support and resource from No.10, MoD, MHCLG, and the Attorney General’s Office, will carry out the government’s commitment to transform the asylum system by:

  • ending the use of hotels to house asylum seekers
  • reducing the number of people supported by the asylum system

It will bring together existing operational teams on decision making and running asylum support and will include a new cross-government team focused on providing alternative accommodation.

role_appointments.title: Dr Rannia Leontaridi OBE FRSA - Director General, Asylum Taskforce

details.body:
The Homeland Security Group sits at the heart of the UK’s national security system, setting strategic direction and playing a crucial enabling role in national security and law enforcement operations.

Its mission is to reduce national security risks to the UK’s people, prosperity and freedoms. It focuses on the highest harm risks to the homeland, whether from terrorists, state actors, or cyber and economic criminals.

The Homeland Security Group:

  • provides system leadership for counter-terrorism and the domestic response to state threats
  • provides joint leadership for countering illicit finance and economic crime
  • maintains and delivers the legal and policy frameworks for some of the most important national security and policing powers, in particular investigatory powers, and delivers crucial investigative capabilities, including communications data
  • facilitates the warrantry for the use of policing and investigatory powers and the oversight of operational partners
  • coordinates the operational response to a domestic national security incident including by facilitating COBR and authorising military assets during an ongoing terrorist incident
  • supports sensitive immigration decisions needed as part of a live incident and on a routine basis

  • makes a critical contribution to harnessing cutting-edge capabilities and building UK cyber power by:

    • leading efforts to deliver next-generation interception of communications
    • providing critical investment in science and technology to anticipate the emerging technologies of the future.

role_appointments.title: Dr Michelle Haslem CMG - Director General, Homeland Security Group

details.body: This new group brings together Home Office science, innovation, data and technology functions to innovate across the whole department and all services.

role_appointments.title: Mike McCarthy - Director General of Digital and Innovation

details.body: Border Force is responsible for securing the UK border and controlling migration at maritime ports and airports across the UK and overseas.

role_appointments.title: Phil Douglas - Director General, Border Force

details.body:
The Border Security Command was established to drive forward the required step change in the UK’s approach to border security, leading our domestic and international effort to deliver a stronger, more unified response to border threats with the immediate focus on countering illegal migration in small boats.

The Border Security Commander oversees the crucial work required to protect the UK border and tackle the smuggling gangs, providing strategic direction and drawing together the work of strategic partners across the border security system including:

  • the National Crime Agency (NCA)
  • intelligence agencies
  • police (NPCC)
  • Immigration Enforcement
  • Border Force

role_appointments.title: Duncan Capps CBE - Interim Border Security Commander

details.body:
The Director General for Immigration Enforcement is responsible for:

  • leading a workforce of approximately 7,200 staff, including a uniformed frontline enforcement capability, and managing an annual budget of around £1.2 billion

  • developing and delivering complex operational programmes to disrupt the criminal gangs that facilitate illegal immigration and exploit vulnerable people

  • tackling illegal migration, removing those with no right to remain in the UK and protecting vulnerable individuals

  • leading teams of immigration officers, criminal investigators and financial investigators, working to tackle organised immigration crime, illegal working and the sale of smuggled goods

  • ensuring the operational effectiveness of casework teams responsible for removing individuals with no right to remain in the UK, including the deportation of foreign national offenders

  • overseeing the immigration detention estate and the escorting contracts delivered through commercial partners

  • delivering the compliant environment, a range of measures designed to prevent those without lawful status from accessing work, benefits and services

  • contributing to cross-departmental governance as Chair of the Home Office People Committee and as a member of the Home Office Executive Committee (ExCo) and other senior governance forums

role_appointments.title: Bas Javid - Director General of Immigration Enforcement

Non-executive directors provide advice and bring an external perspective to the department. They also provide support, guidance and challenge on the progress and implementation of operational business plans, recruitment and succession planning.

details.body:
The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for all Home Office business, including:

  • overarching responsibility for the departmental portfolio
  • oversight of the ministerial team
  • Home Office business in Cabinet
  • membership of the National Security Council (NSC)
  • responsibility for public appointments relating to the work of the department
  • oversight of the Security Service

role.title: Secretary of State for the Home Department

details.body:
• Oversight of all justice portfolios, policy, and Ministry of Justice strategy
  • Oversight of cross-cutting emergency  issues, responses  supported by other  ministers according to  portfolio
  • Oversight of international business and future relations with the EU 
  • MOJ support for the Union
  • Resourcing of the department 
  • Functions of the Lord Chancellor 
  • Judicial policy including pay, pensions and  diversity (these and  other operational  decisions affecting the  judiciary are reserved to the Lord Chancellor)

ordered_roles.title: Secretary of State for the Home Department

details.body:
Responsibilities

Ministry of Justice:

  • victims and witnesses policy (including sponsorship of the Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority)
  • chair of victims panel
  • criminal justice system reform
  • out-of-court disposals
  • criminal law
  • criminal courts procedure
  • veterans review
  • restorative justice
  • miscarriages of justice (including sponsorship of the Criminal Cases Review Commission)

Home Office:

  • crime statistics
  • crime prevention and fighting crime
  • firearms licensing policy
  • public order
  • police funding, pay and pensions
  • police technology
  • Independent Police Complaints Commission
  • police reform and governance
  • national fire policy including fire safety
  • national fire resilience
  • fire funding, pay and pensions

ordered_roles.title: Minister for Policing, Fire and Criminal Justice and Victims

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • international counter-terrorism
  • counter-terrorism operations
  • Pursue, including TPIMs and DWA policy
  • counter-terrorism policing, including section 26 authorisations
  • communications capabilities, including communications data
  • Protect, including critical national infrastructure
  • security exports, Olympic legacy and the 2014 Commonwealth Games
  • counter-terrorism science and technology, including chemical, biological, radioactive, nuclear and explosive
  • space security
  • maritime security
  • Guantanamo detainees and civil litigation
  • Prevent, including extremism task force and RICU
  • mass fatalities, civil contingencies, interoperability
  • aviation security
  • proscription
  • Prepare
  • political and domestic extremism
  • terrorist finance
  • Northern Ireland
  • European Union, including justice and home affairs opt-out
  • special cases, including exclusions, deprivations, national security deportations
  • National Crime Agency
  • serious organised crime
  • cyber crime and security
  • fraud and asset recovery
  • human trafficking, including the Modern Slavery Bill

Responsibilities include all Home Office business in the Lords, including the Serious Crime Bill.

The Parliamentary Under Secretary of State is also responsible for policy on criminal information, including:

  • use of DNA and reform of DNA database
  • CCTV and ANPR
  • Disclosure and Barring Service
  • Security Industry Authority
  • criminal records and criminal information systems
  • criminality information
  • safeguarding vulnerable people
  • Home Office science
  • identity
  • powers of entry
  • statistics and research
  • freedom of information
  • local government and communities
  • sustainability and environmental issues
  • better regulation
  • equality and diversity
  • correspondence champion
  • transparency

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Criminal Information

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • drugs
  • drugs strategy
  • forensic science
  • volume, acquisitive and business crime (including burglary, motor vehicle crime, retail crime, cash vehicles in transit, wildlife/rural crime, metal theft)
  • crime statistics
  • crime strategy
  • designing out crime
  • antisocial behaviour
  • public health
  • alcohol (including Licensing Act and powers of police and local authorities) and alcohol strategy
  • violent crime, violent offender orders
  • homelessness
  • community activism
  • youth crime and youth violence
  • domestic violence, including forced marriage, honour-based killing and FGM
  • sexual violence, sex offenders, child sex offenders review, prostitution, lap dancing
  • violence against women and girls, sexualisation of young people, women’s safety and fear of crime
  • missing persons
  • hate crime
  • child sexual exploitation, including Sexual Violence against Children and Vulnerable People national group
  • animals in science regulation
  • British Transport Police reform
  • firearms

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Crime Prevention

details.body: As International Director, he is responsible for advising the Home Secretary and ministers on immigration and asylum policy and EU justice and home affairs issues, as well as the Home Office’s wider bilateral and multilateral engagement with other countries. This includes work following the UK referendum on EU membership.

ordered_roles.title: International Director and Senior Director for the International and Immigration Policy Group

details.body: Responsible for the government’s strategy, policy and legislative response to the threats of terrorism.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Office for Security and Counter-Terrorism
Director General, Financial and Commercial Group

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • international counter-terrorism
  • terrorism prevention and investigation measures
  • counter-terrorism review/legislation
  • counter-terrorism policing
  • critical national infrastructure
  • industry
  • security exports
  • Home Office science (including counter-terrorism science and technology)
  • mass fatalities and civil contingencies

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Security and Counter Terrorism

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • providing legal advice to ministers and officials across the full range of Home Office business
  • preparing Home Office bills by instructing parliamentary counsel; advising ministers during the passage of the bill through Parliament
  • drafting the Home Office’s subordinate legislation
  • working with the Treasury Solicitor, Treasury Counsel and Attorney General on cases before the domestic courts (including Supreme Court), the European Court of Justice and the European Court of Human Rights making sure, where necessary, that ministers are provided with appropriate legal advice from private law firms and the independent bar

• Supporting the department and agencies in managing their response to reform challenges, so that the organisation and its people remain fully equipped and engaged to meet the Home Office objectives
  • Streamlining the services HR provides for the department and ensuring that HR is more efficient and effective in what it does This will be achieved by:
  • working across boundaries to remove duplication and simplifying the way we do things
  • focusing on performance and productivity
  • being flexible and able to move quickly to make changes

Responsibilities include:

  • developing and overseeing the implementation of the Home Office’s communications strategy
  • increasing the department’s understanding of, and engagement with, the public, its partners and staff
  • building confidence in the department’s ability to protect the public
  • working across government on communications issues and policies
  • contributing to the leadership of the department

The Director General for Immigration Enforcement is responsible for:

  • leading a workforce of approximately 7,200 staff, including a uniformed frontline enforcement capability, and managing an annual budget of around £1.2 billion

  • developing and delivering complex operational programmes to disrupt the criminal gangs that facilitate illegal immigration and exploit vulnerable people

  • tackling illegal migration, removing those with no right to remain in the UK and protecting vulnerable individuals

  • leading teams of immigration officers, criminal investigators and financial investigators, working to tackle organised immigration crime, illegal working and the sale of smuggled goods

  • ensuring the operational effectiveness of casework teams responsible for removing individuals with no right to remain in the UK, including the deportation of foreign national offenders

  • overseeing the immigration detention estate and the escorting contracts delivered through commercial partners

  • delivering the compliant environment, a range of measures designed to prevent those without lawful status from accessing work, benefits and services

  • contributing to cross-departmental governance as Chair of the Home Office People Committee and as a member of the Home Office Executive Committee (ExCo) and other senior governance forums
The group’s core mission is to keep citizens safe, cut crime, protect the vulnerable and reform police, fire and rescue services to improve their efficiency and effectiveness.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Crime, Policing and Fire Group

details.body: The Director General, Capabilities and Resources is responsible for the Home Office’s people, finance and estates, commercial, portfolio management, performance, risk and assurance, security and science functions.

The minister is responsible for:

  • immigration and border policy (including non-EU migration to the UK; family reunion; border security; detention and removal policy; asylum policy; post-Brexit immigration policy for EU nationals)
  • assisting Secretary of State with all policy on exiting the European Union
  • international policy, including EU Justice and Home Affairs
  • border security
  • foreign national offenders (management in immigration detention and deportation to country of origin)
  • resettlement policy (including the operation of Home Office resettlement programmes: Syrian Vulnerable Persons Resettlement Scheme; Gateway; Mandate; and Vulnerable Children’s Resettlement Scheme)
  • implementation of the Immigration Act 2016
  • UK Visas and Immigration
  • Immigration Enforcement
  • Border Force
  • His Majesty’s Passport Office
  • Independent Chief Inspector of Borders and Immigration
  • Home Office immigration transparency data
  • net migration statistics
Border Force is responsible for securing the UK border and controlling migration at maritime ports and airports across the UK and overseas.

details.body: Driving forward the next phase of the Home Office’s transformation, to be consistently excellent. This will support the department in delivering the new government’s priorities and help to drive a strategic approach to the spending review, business planning and the strategic defence and security review.

The Second Permanent Secretary works closely with the Permanent Secretary on all aspects of Home Office leadership, and takes particular responsibility for the borders, immigration and citizenship system.

The minister is responsible for:

  • making the internet a safer place for children by curbing online abuse, exploitation, bullying and access to harmful content
  • promoting safe, open access to the internet for everyone

The minister will be responsible for co-ordinating work across government to resettle up to 20,000 Syrian refugees in the UK. He will advise the Home Secretary, Communities Secretary and Prime Minister on the implementation of the scheme.

He will co-ordinate with local authorities, the devolved administrations and non-governmental organisations on the arrangements for housing and supporting resettled Syrian refugees in the UK. He will also lead engagement with the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees to ensure the UK government supports and assists their resettlement work.

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Countering Extremism)

details.body:
Simon leads the cross-government taskforce on the humanitarian response to the Ukraine refugee crisis.

Simon reports jointly to the Permanent Secretary at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC) and Matthew Rycroft at the Home Office.

The cross-government taskforce is responsible for the delivery of a single end-to-end humanitarian plan.

The role of the taskforce includes:

  • oversight of the UK’s programme of humanitarian assistance for Ukraine
  • working across Whitehall to make sure all departments are supporting the government’s new visa routes and planning for the next steps in the programme
  • undertaking contingency planning across government for possible deterioration of the humanitarian situation
  • making sure there is one consistent narrative for the government’s overall humanitarian response

details.body: Andy Tighe works with, and advises, the Home Secretary and Home Office ministers and senior officials on the strategic development and daily management of all the Home Office’s external and internal communications.

The Border, Immigration and Citizenship System (BICS) is made up of Border Force, Her Majesty’s Passport Office, Immigration Enforcement, UK Visas and Immigration and BICS Policy and Strategy Group (PSG), which provides the policy and strategic structure which underpins the rest of the system.

BICS PSG supports the delivery of the 5 BICS priorities agreed with ministers:

  • managing the implications of Brexit
  • securing the border
  • reducing the illegal migrant population
  • providing world class services and creating prosperity
  • protecting vulnerable people and communities

details.body:
The role of Chief DDaT Officer is to provide exceptionally strong leadership and senior-level advocacy, internally within the Home Office and across government, and externally, for the delivery of digital, data and technology solutions that support the department’s current and future business transformation programmes.

This role demands a deep knowledge of the technology and digital landscape and marketplace, and ensures that the Home Office makes effective use of modern standards and solutions.

details.body:
Responsible for developing, directing and implementing the government’s strategic approach to reducing the risk to the UK from serious and organised crime.

SOCG provides advice on many of the highest harm crimes affecting the UK including child sexual exploitation and abuse, human trafficking, drugs, firearms, cybercrime, fraud and money laundering. The Home Office’s Data and Identity Directorate also sits within SOCG.

The Chief Scientific Adviser is responsible for:

  • providing independent scientific advice to the Home Secretary and other Home Office ministers
  • leading the Home Office Science, Technology, Analysis, Research and Strategy
  • working with the wider network of Chief Scientific Advisers to address cross departmental issues

The Director General for Asylum and Protection is responsible for:

  • running the UK’s asylum system for both adults and unaccompanied children
  • the asylum accommodation system for claimants needing support
  • the UK’s resettlement programmes for refugees
  • other immigration casework including human rights and family-based applications
  • handling of immigration and asylum appeals with HM Courts and Tribunal Service

The Homeland Security Group sits at the heart of the UK’s national security system, setting strategic direction and playing a crucial enabling role in national security and law enforcement operations.

Its mission is to reduce national security risks to the UK’s people, prosperity and freedoms. It focuses on the highest harm risks to the homeland, whether from terrorists, state actors, or cyber and economic criminals.

The Homeland Security Group:

  • provides system leadership for counter-terrorism and the domestic response to state threats
  • provides joint leadership for countering illicit finance and economic crime
  • maintains and delivers the legal and policy frameworks for some of the most important national security and policing powers, in particular investigatory powers, and delivers crucial investigative capabilities, including communications data
  • facilitates the warrantry for the use of policing and investigatory powers and the oversight of operational partners
  • coordinates the operational response to a domestic national security incident including by facilitating COBR and authorising military assets during an ongoing terrorist incident
  • supports sensitive immigration decisions needed as part of a live incident and on a routine basis

  • makes a critical contribution to harnessing cutting-edge capabilities and building UK cyber power by:

    • leading efforts to deliver next-generation interception of communications
    • providing critical investment in science and technology to anticipate the emerging technologies of the future.

The Director General is responsible for:

  • leading on developing, co-ordinating and delivering the government’s strategy, policy and legislation on crime, policing, fire, international law enforcement and judicial cooperation
  • creating the Emergency Services Network, a 4G digital critical communications network which will transform how Britain’s emergency and rescue services operate, and leading various other national digital law enforcement programmes
  • leading the policy and operational teams working on modern slavery and human trafficking, including the UK’s National Referral Mechanism and provision of support for victims in England and Wales

The DG also serves as a member of the Home Office Executive Committee.

Corporate responsibilities

details.body:

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Migration and Borders (Lords Minister)

details.body:
Simon leads the Migration and Borders System.

The Migration and Borders System is made up of:

  • Migration and Borders Group
  • Strategic Operations for Illegal Migration
  • Customer Services
  • Immigration Enforcement
  • Border Force

Simon line manages the Migration and Borders directors general.

The Direct General leads operations required to implement the government’s flagship Illegal Migration Bill and works with other Home Office directors general to ensure that operational action on irregular migration is effectively co-ordinated. The DG is a member of the Home Office Executive Committee (ExCo).

Laura’s responsibilities are:

Minister for Victims and Safeguarding

  • victims and witnesses
  • rape and serious sexual offences (RASSO)
  • violence against women and girls (VAWG)
  • criminal law
  • child sexual abuse
  • miscarriages of justice
  • Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority (CICA)

The non-executive director team come from the private sector with experience of managing large or complex organisations and projects.

Non-executive directors provide advice and bring an external perspective to the department. They also provide support, guidance and challenge on the progress and implementation of operational business plans, recruitment and succession planning.

The ministers responsibilities are:

  • net migration
  • UK points-based system
  • simplifying the immigration system and immigration rules
  • current and future visa policy
  • nationality
  • Windrush
  • Home Office interests in free trade
  • Future Borders and Immigration System and Border Strategy 2025
  • agreements
  • UK Visas and Immigration, HM Passport Office and EU Settlement Scheme
  • asylum decision making (backlog)
  • accommodation (closing hotels)
  • Border Force operations
  • safe and legal routes and resettlement, including:
    • Ukraine Family Scheme
    • Homes for Ukraine Scheme
    • Afghan Citizens Resettlement Scheme
    • Afghan Relocation and Assistance Policy
    • Hong Kong British Nationals (Overseas)
    • Gaza and Israel

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Safeguarding and Violence Against Women and Girls)

details.body: The Senior Responsible Owner for the government’s Safer Streets Mission works across departments to drive delivery and better outcomes.

The Border Security Command was established to drive forward the required step change in the UK’s approach to border security, leading our domestic and international effort to deliver a stronger, more unified response to border threats with the immediate focus on countering illegal migration in small boats.

The Border Security Commander oversees the crucial work required to protect the UK border and tackle the smuggling gangs, providing strategic direction and drawing together the work of strategic partners across the border security system including:

  • the National Crime Agency (NCA)
  • intelligence agencies
  • police (NPCC)
  • Immigration Enforcement
  • Border Force

The Border Security Command was established to drive forward the required step change in the UK’s approach to border security, leading our domestic and international effort to deliver a stronger, more unified response to border threats with the immediate focus on countering illegal migration in small boats.

The Border Security Commander oversees the crucial work required to protect the UK border and tackle the smuggling gangs, providing strategic direction and drawing together the work of strategic partners across the border security system including:

  • the National Crime Agency (NCA)
  • intelligence agencies
  • police (NPCC)
  • Immigration Enforcement
  • Border Force

The role of Permanent Secretary carries with it the responsibility of Principal Accounting Officer, ensuring that the resources authorised by Parliament are used for the purposes authorised by Parliament.

Other responsibilities include:

  • agreeing with the Home Secretary the overarching strategy for delivering ministerial objectives
  • leading, and managing the department to ensure that it operates as effectively and efficiently as possible
  • reporting to the Home Secretary and to Parliament on overall performance

The Minister’s responsibilities are:

  • police accountability and system reform – professional standards, performance, culture  
  • police resourcing and efficiency – neighbourhood policing guarantee, savings, funding 
  • public order, major events, civil contingencies 
  • Emergency Services Mobile Communication Programme 
  • criminal justice system 
  • Early intervention and Young Futures   
  • crime prevention: safer streets, including drugs and county lines, alcohol and licensing, anti-social behaviour, neighbourhood crime, firearms, child criminal exploitation and hate crime.

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Policing and Crime)

details.body:
The Minister’s responsibilities are:

  • Border Security Command: reducing small boat arrivals and organised immigration crime
  • reducing the asylum caseload, exiting hotels and reducing the supported population
  • Immigration Enforcement: Increasing returns (including FNOs), immigration detention estate expansion, illegal working
  • asylum, illegal migration and returns policy, safe and legal policy and refugee resettlement policy
  • reducing net migration: legal migration policy
  • tackling visa abuse
  • innovative solutions
  • Immigration White Paper implementation
  • European Entry Exit System
  • modern slavery policy as it relates to immigration status

details.body:

  • Immigration Rules 
  • Border Force operations 
  • earned settlement and citizenship reform 
  • nationality 
  • Windrush and the Windrush Compensation Scheme 
  • movement of goods across the border
  • UK Visas and Immigration, HM Passport Office and EU Settlement Scheme, General Register Office  
  • oversight of existing safe routes schemes: Ukraine Family Scheme, Homes for Ukraine Scheme, Afghan Citizens Resettlement Scheme, Afghan Relocation and Assistance Policy, Hong Kong British Nationals (Overseas), Gaza and Israel 
  • Future Borders and Immigration System and digital transformation

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Migration and Citizenship)

details.body:
The Asylum Taskforce, with support and resource from No.10, MoD, MHCLG, and the Attorney General’s Office, will carry out the government’s commitment to transform the asylum system by:

  • ending the use of hotels to house asylum seekers
  • reducing the number of people supported by the asylum system

It will bring together existing operational teams on decision making and running asylum support and will include a new cross-government team focused on providing alternative accommodation.
Visas, Passports and Citizenship Services

ordered_roles.title: Director General for Visas, Passports, Citizenship and Resettlement Services

details.body: This new group brings together Home Office science, innovation, data and technology functions to innovate across the whole department and all services.

The non-executive director team come from the private sector with experience of managing large or complex organisations and projects.

Non-executive directors provide advice and bring an external perspective to the department. They also provide support, guidance and challenge on the progress and implementation of operational business plans, recruitment and succession planning.

The non-executive director team come from the private sector with experience of managing large or complex organisations and projects.

Non-executive directors provide advice and bring an external perspective to the department. They also provide support, guidance and challenge on the progress and implementation of operational business plans, recruitment and succession planning.

The Chief Operating Officer Group brings together the key functions required to help the business deliver and includes:  

  • commercial 
  • digital, data and technology 
  • finance 
  • human resources  
  • portfolio and project delivery 
  • security, estates, and information 
  • strategy
